proven.claim: PROVEN: blocks with assertLines.length < 2 are always excluded before self-check
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 261
; PROVEN: blocks with assertLines.length < 2 are always excluded before self-check
; Precondition: assertLines extracted from a proven property's SMT2
; Transition: early-continue guard fires before verifyBlock is called
; Negated: verifyBlock called on a block with fewer than 2 asserts
(declare-const assert_line_count Int)
(declare-const verify_called Int)   ; 1 = verifyBlock invoked
; Code transition: if assertLines.length < 2, continue before verifyBlock
(assert (< assert_line_count 2))
(assert (=> (< assert_line_count 2) (= verify_called 0)))
; Negated property: verifyBlock was called
(assert (= verify_called 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: checkConsistency receives Contract[] from callers with no SMT2 content validation.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 261
; checkConsistency receives Contract[] from callers with no SMT2 content validation.
; Contract.proven[i].smt2 is an arbitrary string — can be malformed.
; verifyBlock on malformed SMT2 returns "error", not "unsat".
; Guard: if (selfResult.result === "unsat") continue
;   → "error" != "unsat" → block is NOT skipped → self-contradictory block included.
; Code transitions:
;   - smt2_valid = 0 (malformed/unparseable content from input contract)
;   - selfResult = "error" (Z3 rejects the SMT2)
;   - guard fires only on "unsat" → block_included = 1
(declare-const smt2_valid Int)       ; 1 = parseable by Z3, 0 = malformed
(declare-const self_result Int)      ; 0=sat, 1=unsat, 2=unknown, 3=error
(declare-const block_included Int)   ; 1 = pushed to namespacedBlocks
; Code transition: malformed SMT2 causes Z3 to return error (3)
(assert (= smt2_valid 0))
(assert (=> (= smt2_valid 0) (= self_result 3)))
; Code transition: guard only excludes on unsat (1)
(assert (=> (= self_result 1) (= block_included 0)))
(assert (=> (not (= self_result 1)) (= block_included 1)))
; Reachable violation: malformed block included in merged consistency check
(assert (= self_result 3))
(assert (= block_included 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: Structural position assumption: slice(0, -1) unconditionally treats the LAST assert
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 261
; Structural position assumption: slice(0, -1) unconditionally treats the LAST assert
; as the negated property to strip. If the last assert is a transition (not a negation),
; stripping it removes a real precondition from the self-check.
; A block whose full precondition set is unsat can then appear sat in the self-check,
; passing the filter and poisoning the merged consistency formula.
; Code transitions:
;   - num_asserts >= 2 (guard already passed)
;   - self_check_asserts = num_asserts - 1 (slice strips last)
;   - last_assert_is_transition = 1 (structural assumption violated)
;   - self_check_is_sat: missing constraint → appears satisfiable
;   - full_precondition_set_is_unsat: with last assert included → contradictory
(declare-const num_asserts Int)
(declare-const self_check_asserts Int)
(declare-const last_assert_is_transition Int)  ; 1 = last assert is a precondition, not negation
(declare-const self_check_unsat Int)           ; 1 = self-check returns unsat
(declare-const full_set_unsat Int)             ; 1 = all asserts together are unsat
(declare-const block_included Int)             ; 1 = pushed to namespacedBlocks
; Code transition: assertLines.length >= 2 (guard already passed)
(assert (>= num_asserts 2))
; Code transition: slice(0, -1) produces num_asserts - 1 precondition asserts
(assert (= self_check_asserts (- num_asserts 1)))
; Scenario: last assert is a real transition constraint, not the negated property
(assert (= last_assert_is_transition 1))
; Code transition: missing the last constraint → self-check is satisfiable
(assert (=> (= last_assert_is_transition 1) (= self_check_unsat 0)))
; But the block's complete precondition set IS self-contradictory
(assert (= full_set_unsat 1))
; Code transition: self_check_unsat = 0 → block is NOT skipped
(assert (=> (= self_check_unsat 0) (= block_included 1)))
; Violation: self-contradictory block included because structural assumption fails
(assert (= block_included 1))
(assert (= full_set_unsat 1))
(check-sat)
